userId.title: Slack User ID
userId.description: The Slack User ID of the user to send the message to.
userId.einstein:description: The Slack User ID of the user to send the message to. A Slack User ID is represented as a string like U12345678. If a string like <@U12345678> is provided, extract a Slack User ID from it by removing the <@ and > characters. For example, the User ID in <@U12345678> is U12345678.

message.title: Slack Message
message.description: Plain text or markdown message to be sent to a user.
message.einstein:description:
Plain text or markdown message to be sent to a user. Do not include HTML tags. You can format markdown lists as ordered or unordered, but DO NOT mix the 2 formats in your message input parameter.
  A string like <@U123456789> represents a User mention in Slack. You can extract a Slack User ID from a mention by removing the <@ and > characters. For example, the User ID in <@U123456789> is U123456789.
  A string like <#C123456789> represents a Channel mention in Slack. You can extract a Slack Channel ID from a mention by removing the <# and > characters. For example, the Channel ID in <@C123456789> is C123456789.
  When providing information to a user, if a Slack User ID is detected in the content being provided, convert that ID into a Slack User @mention. For example if there is a reference to a User in a string like 'U123456789', wrap that string into a string surrounded with  '<@' and  '>', for instance: '<@123456789>'.
  When providing information to a user, if a Slack Channel ID is detected in the content being provided, convert that ID into a Slack Channel @mention. For example if there is a reference to a User in a string like 'U123456789', wrap that string into a string surrounded with  '<#' and  '>', for instance: '<#C123456789>'.
Text like "John Doe" (email: johndoe@example.com slack_user_id: U1234567) is a reference to a Slack user named John Doe with user ID U1234567.
Text quoted by backticks should be sent to Slack verbatim, including the backticks.
User references, including those within text quoted by single-quotes or double-quotes, should be resolved to Slack user ID references (for example, <@U1234567>).
Channel references, including those within text quoted by single-quotes or double-quotes, should be resolved to Slack channel ID references (for example, <#C1234567>).

recommendedActions.title: Recommended Actions
recommendedActions.einstein:description: This input contains list of actions.
